reason: PDF-08 legacy structured-output deletion. The PDF agent is now builder-backed with output_schema: null, explicit generic class-key staging, and `finalize_generic_extraction` materializing canonical backend extraction data. The old JSON-only PdfExtractionResultEnvelope mandate is intentionally gone so curation TSV cannot derive rows from legacy PDF prose/items artifacts.

phrase: Return JSON only, matching `PdfExtractionResultEnvelope`. Use the schema fields rather than adding extra prose-only sections.
reason: PDF-26 legacy structured-output deletion. The current prompt no longer asks the model to populate legacy PDF envelope fields. It requires `list_generic_object_classes`, `stage_generic_object`, and `finalize_generic_extraction`; canonical curation output comes from builder state rather than model-authored JSON/prose sections.

phrase: Use document retrieval tools before answering.
reason: PDF-22 (de-dup). The pre-rewrite <tools> 'Retrieval budget and stopping rules' restated the retrieval-before-output imperative the locked core already mandates as a required tool-call policy ('call at least one document retrieval tool (search_document, read_section, or read_subsection) before final output'). The base prompt no longer restates the imperative; the curator-facing success line 'You retrieved from the document before answering' (PDF-06, kept in <success_criteria>) and the core's required-tool-call policy together carry it.
synonyms: call at least one document retrieval tool (search_document, read_section, or read_subsection) before final output
You retrieved from the document before answering

phrase: search_document: document search across the paper; use default/auto for concepts and lexical-heavy modes for exact controlled tokens
reason: PDF-27. The per-tool capability prose for search_document (blended/auto default for concepts; lexical for exact controlled tokens; section restriction) lives in the search_document bindings.yaml description/summary on the production path. The Phase C skeleton relocates the descriptive tool prose to the tool description; the base <retrieval_strategy> keeps only the curation-relevant 'prefer lexical-heavy search modes for exact controlled tokens' hint.
synonyms: The default blended search combines meaning-based and exact-word matching
Exact-word matching is best for specific gene symbols, strains, alleles, probes, or identifiers

phrase: read_section: read a full top-level section such as Methods or Results; read_subsection: read a targeted subsection when you know where the answer should be
reason: PDF-28. The full-section/full-subsection coverage prose lives in the read_section/read_subsection bindings.yaml summaries ('complete text of every passage in a section, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order ... full coverage'). The base <retrieval_strategy> keeps the curation hint to use read_section/read_subsection for full coverage of a known section, and the descriptive mechanics are relocated to the tool descriptions.
synonyms: complete text of every passage in a section, grouped by the paper's own structure rather than by page order

reason: PDF (search block). The pre-rewrite (HEAD b7e3a415) pdf prompt has NO <search_context>/<search_infrastructure> block: it was already removed in Phase B (commit 24144d52, 'drop duplicated search-infrastructure block from pdf base prompt (now in tool descriptions)'), and the 'Multiple span_ids -> one evidence record' sentence was relocated into <evidence_rules> then. Recorded as a deleted no-op entry for parity with the worked examples so the deletion ledger is explicit; nothing was present to remove in this Phase-C task.
